get.description: Discovery endpoint the login page uses to decide which buttons to show.

post.description: Promote a pending-2FA session to a full session after a valid TOTP code.

get.description:
SVG QR code for the pending enrollment's ``otpauth://`` URI.

404 if the caller has no pending enrollment (never called
``POST /totp/enroll``, or already confirmed one — re-enroll first).
Accepts a full session or a restricted must-enroll (ENROLL) session.

post.description: Confirm TOTP enrollment. Promotes an ENROLL session to a full session.
Start passkey registration (full session or restricted ENROLL session).

schema.title: Response Passkey Register Begin Api V1 Auth Passkey Register Begin Post

post.description: Finish passkey registration. Promotes an ENROLL session to a full session.

schema.title: Response Passkey List Api V1 Auth Passkey Get

delete.description: Delete a passkey. Blocked when it is the last remaining 2FA factor under enforce.

post.description: Start passkey assertion for a pending-2FA session.

schema.title: Response Passkey Authenticate Begin Api V1 Auth Passkey Authenticate Begin Post

post.description: Verify passkey assertion and promote a pending-2FA session to a full session.

get.description:
Kerberos/SPNEGO handshake. On success, 302 to SPA root with a full session.

Per-agent ``sso_eligible`` must be true. SSO is the strong factor — 2FA
is skipped even when the agent has TOTP enrolled.

post.description: Lightweight liveness heartbeat for idle-but-open agent sessions.

get.description: Currently-online agents (Redis TTL presence, resolved via users table).

schema.title: Response List Online Agents Api V1 Agents Online Get
Response List Calendars Api V1 Calendar Calendars Get

get.description: Mint the Znuny-compatible subscription token for the current user.

schema.title: Response Get Feed Token Api V1 Calendar Calendars  Calendar Id  Feed Token Get

get.description: Unauthenticated, token-gated read-only ICS subscription feed.

schema.title: Response List Appointments Api V1 Calendar Appointments Get
Response List Ticket Links Api V1 Calendar Appointments  Appointment Id  Tickets Get
Response List Queues Api V1 Queues Get

post.description: Create a new ticket. Requires ``create`` permission on the queue's group.

get.description:
Open/new counts for tickets owned by the current agent.

Registered before ``/{ticket_id}`` so "my-counts" is not parsed as a
ticket id.

Counts for the dashboard KPI tiles: owned open/new, unclaimed new, escalated.

Registered before ``/{ticket_id}`` so "dashboard-summary" is not parsed as
a ticket id.

Search tickets the current agent may access (``ro`` on the queue).

Matches case-insensitively against ``tn`` and ``title``. Merged/removed
tickets are excluded. Registered before ``/{ticket_id}`` so "search" is
not parsed as a ticket id. Powers "Ticket verknüpfen" / "Ticket
zusammenfassen" pickers.

parameters.name: q

schema.description: Substring matched against ticket number or title
schema.title: Q

parameters.description: Substring matched against ticket number or title

schema.title: Response Search Tickets Api V1 Tickets Search Get

get.description:
Stream every ticket matching the same filters as ``GET /tickets`` as CSV.

Unlike the paginated list endpoint, this has no 200-row cap — rows are
streamed server-side (``TicketService.iter_tickets_for_export``) so
exporting a large queue stays memory-safe. Route registered *before*
``/{ticket_id}`` so FastAPI does not try to parse "export.csv" as a
ticket id.

patch.description:
Apply one or more field mutations to a ticket.

Every mutating field is routed through :class:`TicketWriteService` methods
so per-action Znuny permission keys are enforced (``priority``, ``owner``,
``move_into``, ``rw``, …). Personal watch/unwatch stays ungated.

schema.title: Response List Articles Api V1 Tickets  Ticket Id  Articles Get

post.description:
Add an article to a ticket. Requires ``rw`` permission.

Agent email replies (``channel=email``, ``sender_type=agent``) are SMTP-
delivered then stored (send-then-store). Delivery failure returns HTTP 502
and does not leave a silent no-op 201.

schema.title: Response List Attachments Api V1 Tickets  Ticket Id  Articles  Article Id  Attachments Get
Response Ticket History Api V1 Tickets  Ticket Id  History Get

get.description: Prefilled reply draft (Re: subject, To/Cc, quoted body) for one article.
Response templates (template_type='Answer') for the ticket's queue.

schema.title: Response Ticket Templates Api V1 Tickets  Ticket Id  Templates Get

post.description: Merge ticket_id into main_ticket_id. Requires ``rw`` on both queues.
Forward an article by email (history type 'Forward'). Requires ``rw``.
Bounce (resend) an article verbatim to a new recipient. Requires ``rw``.
Split an article into a new linked ticket. Requires ``rw`` + ``create``.

delete.description:
Hard-delete an internal note. Requires ``rw``.

Customer-visible or non-Internal-channel articles are never deletable and
return HTTP 409 instead.

get.description: List tickets linked to this one.

schema.title: Response List Ticket Links Api V1 Tickets  Ticket Id  Links Get

post.description: Link this ticket to another. Requires ``rw`` on both.

get.description: List form drafts for a ticket (current user only).

schema.title: Response List Drafts Api V1 Tickets  Ticket Id  Drafts Get

put.description: Create or update a draft for (ticket, user, action).

delete.description: Delete a draft for (ticket, user, action).

post.description:
Manual Assist: run the agent synchronously and return the outcome.

Always draft-path (plan §3.4) — never sends a customer-visible article,
regardless of the queue's autonomy setting.

Manual "Zusammenfassen" trigger (plan §3.5) — state-only, never an
article/note. Reuses the same ``note`` permission as Manual Assist since
it is likewise an agent action on the ticket.

schema.title: Response List Processes Api V1 Process  Get

get.description:
A ticket's current process/activity position.

Returns an all-``None``/empty-fields ``TicketProcessStateOut`` (HTTP 200)
if the ticket is not part of any process — that is a normal state, not
an error. A 404 is only raised if the ticket itself does not exist.

post.description:
Submit an activity dialog for *ticket_id*.

Requires ``rw`` on the ticket's queue as a coarse gate — the dialog's own
(finer-grained) ``Permission`` config, if set, is enforced again inside
``engine.submit_activity_dialog`` itself.

get.description:
Server-sent event stream of ``tiqora:events`` pub/sub messages.

Requires an authenticated agent (same session/API-key auth as the rest
of ``/api/v1``). Each message forwarded is the raw JSON payload
published to Redis — see :mod:`tiqora.events.pubsub` for the message
shapes (``ticket_changed`` / ``presence_changed`` /
``ticket_new_in_queue``).

``ticket_new_in_queue`` notifications are filtered to the agent's
readable queues. ``allowed_queue_ids`` is resolved **here**, before the
``StreamingResponse`` is returned, because the DB session dependency's
context manager closes once this function returns — the streaming
generator must not touch it. This snapshots the permission set for the
life of the connection (see the module docstring's v1 limitations).

post.description: Record that ``user`` is viewing/composing on ``ticket_id`` (30s TTL).

get.description: Current viewers/composers on ``ticket_id`` (expired entries drop out via TTL).

schema.title: Response Get Presence Api V1 Tickets  Ticket Id  Presence Get

post.description:
Create a valid customer_user as any authenticated agent.

Mirrors Znuny's AgentTicketCustomer "add customer" — not admin-gated.

schema.title: Response List Priorities Api V1 Reference Priorities Get
Response List States Api V1 Reference States Get

get.description:
Valid agents for owner/responsible pickers.

Kept simple: returns all valid users. Finer per-queue owner scoping (only
agents with ``owner`` permission on the ticket's queue group) can come later.

schema.title: Response List Agents Api V1 Reference Agents Get

get.description: Search valid customer users for the customer-assignment picker.

parameters.name: q

schema.description: Substring matched against login, email, or name
schema.title: Q

schema.title: Response Search Customers Api V1 Reference Customers Get

get.description:
Valid queues the current agent may access, filtered by permission.

``movable=true`` requires ``rw`` (move into the queue). Default is ``ro``.
Always restricted to ``valid_id = 1``.

parameters.name: movable

schema.description: If true, only queues the agent has ``rw`` on (for the Verschieben / move picker). Otherwise queues with at least ``ro``.
schema.title: Movable

parameters.description: If true, only queues the agent has ``rw`` on (for the Verschieben / move picker). Otherwise queues with at least ``ro``.

schema.title: Response List Reference Queues Api V1 Reference Queues Get

get.description:
From-address, signature preview, and rich-text flag for a new-ticket compose form.

Reuses ``queue_outbound_meta``, the same queue lookup the agent-reply send
path (``deliver_agent_email_reply``) resolves From/signature from, so the
preview matches what an actual reply on that queue would show. Agent tags
(``<OTRS_First_Name>`` etc.) are expanded against the current agent, same
as ``TicketService.get_reply_draft``. A not-yet-created ticket has no
ticket/customer context, so ticket_id is omitted and OTRS_TICKET_*/
OTRS_CUSTOMER_DATA_* tags resolve empty here — the real expansion happens
in ``prepare_outgoing_agent_email`` at send time once the ticket exists.

schema.title: Response List Categories Api V1 Kb Categories Get

Response List Articles Api V1 Kb Articles Get

get.description: All KB tags with the count of articles visible to the caller.

schema.title: Response List Tags Api V1 Kb Tags Get

ACL-filtered article bundle selected by tag(s) and/or category.

Intended as the context surface for an agent/LLM: pass a set of tags
and/or a category, get back the readable articles (Markdown included by
default). Content is scoped to the caller's permission groups.

schema.title: Response List Versions Api V1 Kb Articles  Article Id  Versions Get
Response List Attachments Api V1 Kb Articles  Article Id  Attachments Get

Stock Znuny installs with no custom columns return ``[]``. Missing queue → 404.

schema.title: Response List Queue Physical Variables Api V1 Admin Queues  Queue Id  Physical Variables Get

get.description: List customer users. ``page_size`` may be up to 100_000 (``Alle`` UI option).

patch.description:
Apply ``valid_id`` and/or ``customer_id`` to many customer_user rows.

Bound a single call via the schema (1 ≤ len(ids) ≤ 1000). Stamps
change_time/change_by and invalidates the same Znuny cache types as the
single-row update path.

get.description:
Customer users with extra visibility into *customer_id* — reverse of
customer-user↔companies (Znuny ``customer_user_customer``).

Distinct from users whose *primary* ``customer_user.customer_id`` is this
company; those are not listed here.

schema.title: Response Get Customer Company Users Api V1 Admin Customer Companies  Customer Id  Customer Users Get

get.description:
Companies *customer_user_login* has additional ticket visibility into —
the Customer-User↔Customers editor's read side.

This is the Znuny ``customer_user_customer`` M2M (extra visibility), keyed
by the customer-user *login*. It is distinct from the user's single primary
``customer_user.customer_id`` (their home company), which is edited on the
Customer Users page instead.

schema.title: Response Get Customer User Companies Api V1 Admin Customer Users  Customer User Login  Companies Get

put.description:
Grant *customer_user_login* additional visibility into *customer_id*'s
tickets (Znuny ``customer_user_customer`` — distinct from the user's
primary ``customer_user.customer_id``).

Groups the customer user has full (``rw``) access to — the
Customer-User↔Groups editor's read side.

Znuny stores the customer-user identity as the *login string* in
``group_customer_user.user_id`` (not the numeric ``customer_user.id``).
The editor toggles ``rw`` (see :func:`assign_customer_user_group`), so the
read set is filtered to that key — same pattern as agent↔group.

schema.title: Response Get Customer User Groups Api V1 Admin Customer Users  Login  Groups Get

put.description:
Grant *login* the given permission on *group_id*.

Upserts into Znuny ``group_customer_user`` (composite identity:
login + group_id + permission_key). ``permission_value`` is required by
the table (unlike agent ``group_user``).

get.description:

* ``side=attachments`` — rows in ``standard_template_attachment``

inserts one row per id in ``attachment_ids`` (deduplicated, order-

attachment editor needs — single-add PUT alone cannot clear unchecked
rows the way a replace body can.

Soft-deactivate (``valid = False``); matches the invariant used by
other admin CRUD resources (queues, states, ...) that are never hard-deleted.

``/preview``, but resolves ids only — no customer/sample/count breakdown).

Per-customer before/after preview. Read-only: no commit is ever issued,
and the session is rolled back explicitly in case the engine's read helpers
ever pick up a pending flush.

List queue variables.

* ``queue_id`` set → only that queue's rows (not globals).
* ``global_only=true`` → only ``queue_id IS NULL`` rows.
* both omitted → all rows.

post.description:
Dry-run a sample tool result against escalation rules (plan block 6).

Pure function of the request body — nothing is read from or written to
the database; validation and matching reuse :mod:`tiqora.ai.escalation`
exactly as the runtime guard does.

schema.title: Response List Ai Acl Api V1 Admin Ai Acl Get

delete.description:
Hard-delete a draft, any status — admin cleanup, not part of the
normal lifecycle (see ``tiqora.ai.drafts`` module docstring).

post.description:
Canonical always-available SOAP provider endpoint.

Unlike REST, SOAP has no per-operation route mapping in Znuny — a single
endpoint per webservice accepts any operation, dispatched from the SOAP
Body wrapper element name. This fallback route uses the default Znuny
``NameSpace`` (``http://www.otrs.org/TicketConnector/``); webservices
configured in ``gi_webservice_config`` with their own NameSpace are
additionally mounted at ``/Webservice/{name}`` and ``/WebserviceID/{id}``
(see :func:`_load_soap_webservices` / :func:`build_soap_router`).
Re-mount dynamic webservice routes without restart (authenticated).

schema.title: Response Admin Reload Routes Znuny Compat Admin Reload Post

get.description: Only articles with ``is_visible_for_customer == 1`` — internal notes never returned.

schema.title: Response List Articles Api Portal Tickets  Ticket Id  Articles Get

get.description: Sanitised article body — 404 unless owned by the customer and customer-visible.

Resolve a rendered article body's ``cid:`` image reference.

Same ownership + ``is_visible_for_customer`` scoping as the numeric
attachment endpoint below; this is what ``rewrite_cid_urls`` targets for
portal-rendered article bodies (see ``domain.article_html``).
Liveness probe — process is up.
Readiness probe — critical dependencies reachable.
Prometheus metrics exposition.

ActivityDialogDetailOut.title: ActivityDialogDetailOut
ActivityDialogDetailOut.description: Full field definitions for one activity dialog.

ActivityDialogFieldOut.title: ActivityDialogFieldOut
ActivityDialogFieldOut.description:
One field definition within :class:`ActivityDialogDetailOut`, for the
frontend to build a dynamic form.

ActivityDialogRefOut.title: ActivityDialogRefOut
ActivityDialogRefOut.description:
A dialog reference within :class:`ProcessDetailOut` — entity id + name
only, no field-level detail (that is ``ActivityDialogDetailOut``, fetched
per-dialog by the frontend as needed).

ActivityDialogSubmitOut.title: ActivityDialogSubmitOut
ActivityDialogSubmitOut.description:
Outcome of submitting an activity dialog, mirroring
:class:`tiqora.process.engine.ActivityDialogSubmitResult`, plus the
freshly re-fetched ticket process state so the frontend can immediately
re-render without a second round-trip.

ActivityDialogSummaryOut.title: ActivityDialogSummaryOut
ActivityDialogSummaryOut.description: A candidate activity dialog available at a ticket's current activity.

AgentCustomerCreateOut.title: AgentCustomerCreateOut
AgentCustomerCreateOut.description: Created customer-user ref for the ticket Kunde dialog.

AgentCustomerCreateRequest.title: AgentCustomerCreateRequest
AgentCustomerCreateRequest.description:
Body for agent-side customer-user creation (Znuny AgentTicketCustomer).

No password — agents create the contact record; portal auth is separate.

ApiKeyCreated.title: ApiKeyCreated
ApiKeyCreated.description:
Create response: includes the plaintext ``key`` exactly once.

The plaintext is never stored and cannot be retrieved again — copy it
immediately after creation.

ApiKeyOut.title: ApiKeyOut
ApiKeyOut.description: Public API-key metadata. Never includes key_hash or plaintext.

AssignableGroup.title: AssignableGroup
AssignableGroup.description: A permission group the current user may assign to a KB category.

AttachmentOut.title: AttachmentOut
AttachmentOut.description: Metadata for a KB article attachment (content fetched via download route).

AttachmentRefOut.title: AttachmentRefOut
AttachmentRefOut.description: Slim attachment row for assignment editors (no blob).

AuthConfigAgentOut.title: AuthConfigAgentOut
AuthConfigAgentOut.description: One agent row for the admin auth-config list.

CustomerUserBulkUpdate.title: CustomerUserBulkUpdate
CustomerUserBulkUpdate.description: Bulk-patch a set of customer_user rows (validity and/or company).

CustomerUserGroupAssignment.title: CustomerUserGroupAssignment
CustomerUserGroupAssignment.description:
Assign a customer-user (by login) to a group with ro/rw permission.

Znuny ``group_customer_user`` stores one row per (login, group, key) with
``permission_value`` (unlike agent ``group_user``, which has no value
column). Customer-user group grants are only ``ro`` / ``rw``.

DaemonServiceOut.title: DaemonServiceOut
DaemonServiceOut.description: One row of the daemon catalog with effective schedule + live status.

DaemonUpdate.title: DaemonUpdate
DaemonUpdate.description:
``enabled``/``interval_seconds`` null or omitted leaves the field
untouched; ``interval_seconds=0`` or ``null`` (when explicitly sent)
clears the DB override and reverts to the config default.

DashboardSummary.title: DashboardSummary
DashboardSummary.description: KPI-tile counts for the agent dashboard.

ErasureSelectorIn.title: ErasureSelectorIn
ErasureSelectorIn.description: Combinable AND filter for customer_user resolution.

EscalationTestIn.title: EscalationTestIn
EscalationTestIn.description:
Dry-run input for the escalation-rule tester (nothing is persisted).

``rules_json`` is the same JSON-array text the policy editor holds in its
``escalation_rules`` field; ``sample_json`` is a sample raw tool result.

FollowUpPossibleOut.title: FollowUpPossibleOut
FollowUpPossibleOut.description:
Reference row for resolving ``queue.follow_up_id`` to a name.

Stock Znuny names: ``possible``, ``reject``, ``new ticket``.

GdprCustomerRecordPreviewRequest.title: GdprCustomerRecordPreviewRequest
GdprCustomerRecordPreviewRequest.description:
Per-customer before/after preview request. Login is body-only because
logins may contain characters (e.g. ``#``) that are unsafe as a path segment.

GdprDeleteSummaryRowOut.title: GdprDeleteSummaryRowOut
GdprDeleteSummaryRowOut.description: One row/object count that would be removed by a delete-mode erasure.

GdprErasureJobCreate.title: GdprErasureJobCreate
GdprErasureJobCreate.description: Only destructive entry point — requires confirm=true.

GdprFieldPreviewOut.title: GdprFieldPreviewOut
GdprFieldPreviewOut.description: One before/after row in the per-customer record preview (anonymize mode).

GdprSelectorCountOut.title: GdprSelectorCountOut
GdprSelectorCountOut.description: Fast match count for the selector live-counter (no joins/samples).

GdprSelectorCountRequest.title: GdprSelectorCountRequest
GdprSelectorCountRequest.description: Live match-count request — shares the selector shape with preview/apply.

KnowledgeArticle.title: KnowledgeArticle
KnowledgeArticle.description: One article in a knowledge bundle handed to an agent/LLM.

KnowledgeBundle.title: KnowledgeBundle
KnowledgeBundle.description: ACL-filtered set of articles selected by tag(s) and/or category.

MailOutboundTestIn.title: MailOutboundTestIn
MailOutboundTestIn.description: Optional recipient; when set a short test message is sent after AUTH.

MyTicketCounts.title: MyTicketCounts
MyTicketCounts.description: Owned-ticket counts for the "My tickets" sidebar badges.

OccurrenceOut.title: OccurrenceOut
OccurrenceOut.description: A single expanded occurrence within a queried date range.

PasskeyStatusOut.title: PasskeyStatusOut
PasskeyStatusOut.description: Returned after register/finish (and optionally after delete).

PhysicalQueueVariableOut.title: PhysicalQueueVariableOut
PhysicalQueueVariableOut.description:
A non-standard column on the Znuny ``queue`` table (site-specific patch).

Surfaced read-only so admins can see physical values that resolve as
``<OTRS_QUEUE_{name}>`` even when no ``tiqora_queue_variable`` row exists.

PostmasterFilterOut.title: PostmasterFilterOut
PostmasterFilterOut.description: Grouped by ``f_name`` (Znuny's filter identity is the name, not a single row).

PostmasterFilterWrite.title: PostmasterFilterWrite
PostmasterFilterWrite.description:
Create/replace body for a named PostMaster filter.

Znuny stores one ``postmaster_filter`` row per Match/Set entry under the
same ``f_name``; ``stop`` maps to ``f_stop`` on every row for that name.

PostmasterMatchRuleIn.title: PostmasterMatchRuleIn
PostmasterMatchRuleIn.description: One Match condition (email header + regex/value, optional negate).

PostmasterSetRuleIn.title: PostmasterSetRuleIn
PostmasterSetRuleIn.description: One Set action (typically an X-OTRS-* header + value).

ProcessDetailOut.title: ProcessDetailOut
ProcessDetailOut.description:
A single process's full graph: activities and their dialogs.

Transition/condition internals are intentionally not exposed here — the
frontend does not need them to render a process, and Tiqora does not
leak internal BPM routing logic to clients (see ``TicketProcessStateOut``).

RecurrenceIn.title: RecurrenceIn
RecurrenceIn.description: Common RRULE subset: FREQ + INTERVAL + (COUNT xor UNTIL).

ReplyDraftOut.title: ReplyDraftOut
ReplyDraftOut.description: Prefilled reply/reply-all draft for one article (see quoting.py).

StandardAttachmentOut.title: StandardAttachmentOut
StandardAttachmentOut.description: Attachment master row; ``content`` is base64-encoded binary.

StateTypeOut.title: StateTypeOut
StateTypeOut.description: Reference row for resolving ``ticket_state.type_id`` to a name.

SubjectConfigOut.title: SubjectConfigOut
SubjectConfigOut.description: Effective subject-hook config plus Znuny baseline and raw overrides.

SubjectConfigUpdate.title: SubjectConfigUpdate
SubjectConfigUpdate.description:
Upsert Tiqora overrides. Empty string / null clears an override.

``enabled`` null clears the override (reverts to default True).
``subject_format`` must be Left, Right, or None when set.

SubjectHookOverridesOut.title: SubjectHookOverridesOut
SubjectHookOverridesOut.description: Raw Tiqora overrides; ``None`` means the field is not overridden.

SubjectHookZnunyOut.title: SubjectHookZnunyOut
SubjectHookZnunyOut.description: Underlying Znuny SysConfig values (never written by Tiqora).

SystemAddressOut.title: SystemAddressOut
SystemAddressOut.description:
Reference row for resolving ``queue.system_address_id`` to a label.

Znuny ``system_address``: ``value0`` is the email, ``value1`` the real name.
UI display is typically ``"{value1} <{value0}>"``.

TagOut.title: TagOut
TagOut.description: A KB tag with the count of articles visible to the requesting user.

TemplateAttachmentsReplace.title: TemplateAttachmentsReplace
TemplateAttachmentsReplace.description: Full replacement set of attachments for a standard template.

TicketPermissions.title: TicketPermissions
TicketPermissions.description:
Effective Znuny permission keys for the ticket's queue group.

Each flag is true when the agent holds that key **or** ``rw`` (which
implies every key). Used by the agent UI to disable per-action controls.

TicketProcessStateOut.title: TicketProcessStateOut
TicketProcessStateOut.description:
A ticket's current process/activity position, for the REST layer.

All fields are ``None``/empty when the ticket is not part of any
process — that is a normal state (HTTP 200), not an error; see
``GET /api/v1/process/ticket/{ticket_id}/state``.

``available_transitions_count`` is informational only (how many outgoing
transitions the current activity has) — the transitions' own
conditions/actions are not exposed to avoid leaking internal BPM routing
logic to the client.

TicketSearchHitOut.title: TicketSearchHitOut
TicketSearchHitOut.description: Compact ticket hit for agent link/merge pickers.
